# P-A-002 — Split tender (slicing)

> Découpage de marché (saucissonnage)

## Description (EN)

Multiple awards to the same supplier in a short window, each just below the open-tender threshold.

## Description (FR)

Plusieurs marchés au même fournisseur dans une fenêtre courte, chacun juste sous le seuil d'appel d'offres ouvert.

<!-- END auto-generated -->

## Detection logic

Detects threshold-avoidance splitting: N awards to the same supplier within a short window (default 90 days), each individually below the open-tender threshold (≥ 30 M XAF), summing above it. Cluster-strength scales with both the count of split lots and how close to the threshold each individual amount sits.

## Likelihood-ratio reasoning

Splitting a single project into below-threshold lots to bypass the open-tender requirement is the textbook bypass identified in OECD-IBC §3.2 and WB-PRG §5.04(b). Cameroon law (Décret 2018/366, art. 9) makes any anti-competitive splitting an explicit ground for nullity; the pattern is therefore not just a fraud signal but a legal-status indicator. Default prior 0.30 + weight 0.8 reflect the architect's strong-signal judgement, calibratable downward if the ECE sweep finds frequent benign cases (e.g. genuinely independent small projects awarded sequentially).

## Known false-positive traps

- **Genuinely independent small projects.** A ministry may legitimately award N small projects to the same supplier (catering for separate events, multiple-site repairs). Distinguishing requires scope-analysis of each contract, which is downstream of this signal.
- **Framework-agreement draws.** A pre-qualified framework allows multiple call-offs to the same supplier without re-tendering. Cameroon's 2018 framework reform makes these explicit; if `procurement_method` is `framework`/`accord-cadre`, the pattern should de-weight (currently does not — calibration TODO).
- **End-of-fiscal-year clustering.** Q4 budget execution sometimes produces a flurry of small awards by absorption budgétaire pressure. Treat the pattern as a flag for review, not a determinant.

## Production wiring

Requires `worker-extractor` to populate `supplier_name` + `amount_xaf` on every award, and `worker-entity` to deduplicate aliases so the cluster query uses the canonical supplier id. Verify deduplication via `entity.canonical.id` in the resolved subject.
